title: Eat more organs: Homocysteine and cardiovascular disease

chunks.title: Introduction to Homocysteine
chunks.summary: The speaker introduces homocysteine, discussing its relevance to nutrition and health. They explain their personal connection to the topic, having a specific genetic polymorphism related to the mthfr gene.
chunks.transcript: homocysteine i want to talk about this one for one moment uh again this may get a little complex here homocysteine is something i've talked about in the past i've done entire podcasts on

chunks.title: The Role of Folate and MTHFR
chunks.summary: A detailed explanation of dietary folate, distinguishing between folate found in nature and synthetic folic acid. The speaker discusses the enzymatic processes involving mthfr and its implications for those with genetic variations.
chunks.transcript: homocysteine and on mthfr i am italian i am homozygous for the 677cdt polymorphism in the mthfr gene which is part of this methionine or folate cycle you can see here dietary folate not folic acid that is not occurring in nature it is tetrahydrofolate or methylfolate or adenosil

chunks.title: Personal Experience with Homocysteine Levels
chunks.summary: The speaker shares their personal experiences with homocysteine levels in relation to dietary habits, particularly their intake of liver and egg yolks. They highlight how these food sources impact their homocysteine levels.
chunks.transcript: folate come in through the diet okay they end up as tetrahydrofolate there's a series of enzymatic reactions which turn them into 510 methylene tetrahydrofolate that is acted upon by mthfr methylene tetrahydrofolate reductase which needs riboflavin that is b2 and b3 and dha according to this diagram do-cos and hexanoic acid and that makes 5-methylfolate now in those of us italians hispanics tend to have this polymorphism this mthfr enzyme doesn't work really well or it works more slowly which means we either need to get more folate into our bodies or we need to find some source of almethylfolate or we need to get more riboflavin so this is a really important thing at the time of this blood work i was traveling i wasn't getting as much liver as i usually do and i wasn't eating as many egg yolks as i usually do this is probably why my homies have seen bumped the last few homosystems i've seen have been seven when i'm eating enough raw liver per day an ounce half an ounce a day that keeps my homocysteine at seven with no supplementation of almethylfolate in my diet now is

chunks.title: Homocysteine as a Cardiovascular Risk Factor
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses the significance of homocysteine in cardiovascular health and the potential risks associated with high levels. They reference current research on the role of vitamins like riboflavin in maintaining healthy homocysteine levels.
chunks.transcript: homocysteine the end-all and be-all of the cardiovascular risk equation no it's not we don't fully understand it i don't think but i think it is an important thing to know about and if your homocysteine i believe is over eight i would increase the amount of riboflavin in your diet where do you get riboflavin liver and heart are the best sources of riboflavin it's a good review paper i found the current status of homocysteine as a risk factor for cardiovascular disease a mini review is from 2018 and you can see here in the abstract they say that treatment of hyper homocysteine emia with folic acid that should be folate folic acid is and b vitamins specifically riboflavin

chunks.title: Dietary Recommendations for Managing Homocysteine
chunks.summary: Recommendations are provided for individuals with elevated homocysteine, emphasizing the importance of incorporating organ meats and egg yolks into the diet. The speaker notes the observed benefits of organ consumption on their own health.
chunks.transcript: but also b3 prevents the development b3 is niacin prevents the development of atherosclerosis cardiovascular disease and strokes so it is important to treat an elevated homocysteine and i've seen homocysteine as high as 18 or 20 in people who are very low on folate or riboflavin because they're not eating enough of these things now there's some controversy about how much cardiovascular effect it has but i've seen very clear responses in my diet to more organs and a little more egg yolks my homocysteine i want to see it below eight i was talking to a friend this morning he said his dad's almost 16 is 12 or more what does his dad do his dad does basically a carnivore diet of just meat of course your homocysteine is going to be 12 if you have polymorphisms in the mthfr enzyme i think that this is an indication that more organs like heart and liver should be added back to the diet either desiccated or fresh
